sutta: 1 morning while the exalted 1 dwelt near sabahti and anatha pindika's park in jaita grove the variable sariputta roped himself early and taking bowl and rope entered savarti for arms now the variable sarikuta thought thus too soon still is it to visit savati for arms what if i were to go to the park of the wondrous of other views and he approached their palm and having come treated them exchanged the usual polite talk with them and sat down at 1 side now at that time this remark by chance arose among them as they said assembled reverences whosoever lives the holy life in its fullness and purity for 12 years it is right to say of him the monk is praiseworthy and the venerable sarikuta neither praised nor scorned their words but got up and departed thinking i shall learn the truth of this talk from the exalted 1 then when he had gone his round for arms in sabahti and returned and eaten his meal he approached the exalted 1 saluted and sat down at 1 side and so seated he told the exalted 1 all that had occurred adding is it possible lord in this dhamma vinaya to declare among praiseworthy just merely on the score of years no sariputta it is not these 7 grounds for praise have been taught by me who have realized the matter by personal knowledge what 7 consider the monk who is keenly eager to undergo the training and whose zeal reigns not in the days of his training who is keenly eager to observe dhamma and whose zeal reigns not in the days of his training to discipline desire and who zeal wins not in the days of his training to go apart and whose zeal reigns not in the days of his training to put forth energy and whose zeal wins not in the days of his training to master mindfulness and whose zeal wins not in the days of his training to attain right view and whose zeal reigns not in the days of his training beverly sarikuta these are the 7 grounds for praise taught by me who have realized the matter by personal knowledge indeed sarikuta if among possessed of these 7 grounds live the holy life in its fullness and purity for 12 years it is right to say of him the monk is praiseworthy if he lived the holy life for 24 years for 36 years or for 8 48 years it is right to say of him the monk is praiseworthy because the end of the sutta
commentary:
so here the buddha is trying to say that among is not only praiseworthy because of the seniority the number of years he raised the rope but there are other qualities which would make among praiseworthy that he's keen to train in the holy life and then his seal of his diligence does not lessen as the years go by and then he observes dhamma or he lives his life according to dhamma and he carries on the same way as the years go by and then he disciplines his desire and as the years go by he does not lessen his discipline and then he goes apart or he's a bit aloof from others and his he wins not know as the years go by and then his diligent he puts forth energy and he carries on as the years go by and then he masters mindfulness and he attains right view etc and he's the important thing is that he continues in that same way diligently year after year [Music] sometimes a monk may have a good start but there's a monks they have a bad ending they this rope or they break the precept and then they are false to this rope etc so if a manga has these qualities as well as seniority then he is place worthy
